      "それは秩序への欲求を持っています。",
      "それは哲学における理性への無条件の執着を持っています。",

      "それは政治における権威に基づく体制を目指しています。",

      "それはフランス社会が退廃と腐敗によって損なわれているという信念を特徴としています。",

      "それは理性への無条件の執着を特徴としています。",
      "それはロマン主義に対する古典主義の擁護を特徴としています。"

About: It is about absolute integral nationalism, monarchism, corporatism, national syndicalism, and opposition to democracy, liberalism, capitalism, and communism.
Affects: It affects the cohesion of France and its greatness.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Action française movement.
CreatedBy: It was created by Charles Maurras.
CreatedTo: It was created to affirm the cohesion of France and its greatness.
HasA: It has a postulate of patriotism.
It has a desire for order.
It has an unconditional attachment to reason in philosophy.
It has a defense of classicism in art.
It has a regime based on authority in politics.
It has a respect for regional liberties.
It has institutions charged with guaranteeing regional freedoms and maintaining national unity.
It has the magisterium of the Catholic Church in morality.
It has a political model based on the "Real Country".
It has a distinction between the "Real Country" and the "Legal Country".
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by a counter-revolutionary doctrine.
It is characterized by a slogan "Politics first".
It is characterized by a postulate of patriotism.
It is characterized by a belief that French society was undermined by decadence and corruption.
It is characterized by philosophical influences from Plato, Aristotle, Joseph de Maistre, Dante, Thomas Aquinas, and Auguste Comte.
It is characterized by historical influences from Sainte-Beuve, Fustel de Coulanges, Hippolyte Taine, and Ernest Renan.
It is characterized by the belief that the "anti-France" consisted of Jews, Protestants, freemasons, and foreigners.
It is characterized by a desire for order.
It is characterized by an unconditional attachment to reason.
It is characterized by a defense of classicism against romanticism.
Influenced: It influenced the Révolution nationale of the Vichy regime.
It influenced the regime of Antonio Salazar in Portugal.
It influenced the regime of Francisco Franco in Spain.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by Plato.
It was influenced by Aristotle.
It was influenced by Joseph de Maistre.
It was influenced by Dante.
It was influenced by Thomas Aquinas.
It was influenced by Auguste Comte.
It was influenced by Sainte-Beuve.
It was influenced by Fustel de Coulanges.
It was influenced by Hippolyte Taine.
It was influenced by Ernest Renan.
IsA: It is a synthesis between counter-revolutionary ideas and nationalism.
It is a school of thought.
KnownFor: It is known for advocating absolute integral nationalism.
It is known for advocating monarchism.
It is known for advocating corporatism.
It is known for advocating national syndicalism.
It is known for opposing democracy.
It is known for opposing liberalism.
It is known for opposing capitalism.
It is known for opposing communism.
It is known for its "organizing empiricism".
It is known for its distinction between the "Real Country" and the "Legal Country".
UsedFor: It is used for ensuring national cohesion.
It is used for guaranteeing regional freedoms.
It is used for maintaining national unity.
It is used for promoting order.
It is used for promoting reason.
It is used for promoting classicism.
It is used for establishing a regime based on authority.
It is used for respecting regional liberties.
It is used for unifying the nation through the Catholic Church.
It is used for distinguishing the "Real Country" from the "Legal Country".
UsedIn: It is used in intellectual and student circles in France.
It is used in law departments.
It is used in medical departments.
UsedTo: It is used to be a counter-revolutionary doctrine.
It is used to affirm the cohesion of France.
It is used to affirm the greatness of France.
It is used to oppose democracy.
It is used to oppose liberalism.
It is used to oppose capitalism.
It is used to oppose communism.
It is used to exclude "anti-France" from the nation.
It is used to exalt the national interest.
It is used to distinguish the "Real Country" from the "Legal Country".
